title: Civil BIM: Connecting Site Infrastructure to Building BIM in Revit
content:
Civil infrastructure — roads, drainage, utilities, site grading — intersects with building BIM at the foundation level and at every underground service connection. Managing this boundary between civil and architectural-structural BIM is a persistent source of coordination gaps.

Why This Matters

Underground service clashes between civil drainage and building foundations are among the most expensive to resolve. They are discovered during earthworks when structural elements are already cast and drainage redesign requires contractor claim negotiations.

Practical Guidance

Civil 3D to Revit Exchange: Civil 3D site geometry (road alignments, grading surfaces, drainage networks) can be exported to Revit as linked files or imported as reference geometry. Use AutoCAD Civil 3D's Revit interoperability tools or IFC to bring site boundary and underground service positions into the building coordination model.

Shared Coordinate Alignment: Civil models use a national grid coordinate system. Revit building models use a project-based shared coordinate. The transformation between these two systems must be precisely defined and applied before any civil-building coordination clash detection is run.

Underground Service Zones: Model civil drainage, water supply, and utility routes as 3D elements in the coordination model — not as 2D drawings. Only 3D elements participate in Navisworks clash detection. A 2D utility drawing referenced as an image is invisible to clash detection.

Handover Boundary: Define clearly where civil BIM responsibility ends and building BIM begins: at the building envelope, at the basement slab, or at the service entry point. This boundary determines who models the transition zone and who is responsible for coordination clashes within it.

Checklist

  • Import civil geometry as 3D linked file or IFC — not as 2D reference
  • Define and document coordinate transformation between national grid and project coordinate
  • Model underground services as 3D elements before foundation design freeze
  • Define civil-to-building BIM handover boundary in BIM Execution Plan

LUA BIM LABS Insight

The civil-building boundary is where coordinate systems meet and clash detection blind spots occur — eliminate 2D references in this zone before coordination begins.
